title: The Risks and Rewards of Using AI in Modern Healthcare
subtitle: As hospitals adopt new technology, experts warn about the dangers of losing the human touch and the risk of inaccurate medical records.

summary.en: Artificial Intelligence is changing how doctors work, offering both efficiency and new dangers. While some AI tools help doctors focus on patients, others create risks by making up false information. Experts worry that patients might form unhealthy emotional bonds with AI agents instead of real people. A recent audit in Ontario found that many AI tools used for taking notes are often inaccurate. Balancing technology with human connection remains a major challenge for the future of medicine.
summary.zh: 人工智慧正在改變醫師的工作方式，既帶來效率也產生了新的危險。雖然部分人工智慧工具能幫助醫師專注於病患，但其他工具卻因捏造錯誤資訊而帶來風險。專家擔心病患可能會與人工智慧代理人建立不健康的情感連結，而非與真人互動。安大略省近期的一項審計發現，許多用於記錄筆記的人工智慧工具往往不夠準確。在科技與人際連結之間取得平衡，仍是未來醫學面臨的一大挑戰。

paragraphs.en: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is quickly changing the world of medicine. Many doctors are now using new technology to help them manage their daily work. While these tools offer exciting possibilities, they also bring serious risks that healthcare providers and patients must consider. From taking notes during appointments to talking with patients between visits, AI is becoming a common part of the medical experience.
paragraphs.zh: 人工智慧（AI）正在迅速改變醫學領域。許多醫師現在利用新科技來協助處理日常工作。雖然這些工具提供了令人振奮的可能性，但也帶來了醫療提供者與病患必須審慎考量的嚴重風險。從看診時的筆記記錄，到回診間隔期間與病患的對話，人工智慧正逐漸成為醫療體驗中常見的一部分。

paragraphs.en: According to STAT News, some doctors are already using AI to improve efficiency. One helpful tool is called "ambient listening." This technology records conversations between doctors and patients and automatically turns them into written notes. This allows the doctor to look at the patient instead of a computer screen. When used this way, AI can make the time spent in the doctor’s office feel more personal and focused.
paragraphs.zh: 根據 STAT News 報導，一些醫師已經開始使用人工智慧來提升效率。其中一項實用的工具稱為「環境聆聽」。這項技術能錄下醫師與病患之間的對話，並自動將其轉化為書面筆記。這讓醫師能夠注視病患，而非盯著電腦螢幕。以這種方式使用時，人工智慧能讓診間的看診時間感覺更具個人化且更專注。

paragraphs.en: However, there are concerns about how accurate these tools really are. Ars Technica reports that a government audit in Ontario found major problems with AI note-taking tools. The audit tested 20 different AI systems and found that all of them made mistakes. Some systems created "hallucinations," which means the AI invented information that was never said. Other systems recorded the wrong names for medicines or missed important details about a patient's mental health. The auditor general warned that these errors could lead to harmful treatment plans and negatively affect a patient’s health.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，這些工具的準確性仍令人擔憂。Ars Technica 報導指出，加拿大安大略省的一項政府審計發現，人工智慧筆記工具存在重大問題。該審計測試了 20 種不同的人工智慧系統，發現每一種都會出錯。有些系統會產生「幻覺」，意即人工智慧捏造了從未說過的資訊。其他系統則記錄了錯誤的藥物名稱，或遺漏了關於病患心理健康的重要細節。審計長警告，這些錯誤可能導致有害的治療計畫，並對病患的健康造成負面影響。

paragraphs.en: Beyond the accuracy of notes, experts are also worried about how AI interacts with patients. In the field of addiction medicine, some companies are creating "conversational agents." These are AI programs that text patients to check on their progress. While this could help doctors keep track of a patient’s recovery, STAT News notes that some companies boast about patients forming emotional relationships with these AI agents. This is a major concern for many medical professionals.
paragraphs.zh: 除了筆記的準確性之外，專家也對人工智慧與病患互動的方式感到憂心。在成癮醫學領域，一些公司正在開發「對話代理人」。這些是透過傳送簡訊來追蹤病患進度的人工智慧程式。雖然這有助於醫師掌握病患的康復狀況，但 STAT News 指出，有些公司甚至吹噓病患會與這些人工智慧代理人建立情感連結。這對許多醫療專業人員來說是一個重大的隱憂。

paragraphs.en: In addiction recovery, human connection is very important. Patients often rely on therapists, family members, and support groups to stay healthy. If a patient starts to feel a deep emotional bond with an AI program, they might stop reaching out to the real people who can help them. According to STAT News, doctors believe that patients have a limited amount of emotional energy. If they spend that energy on an AI agent, they might have less to give to their actual support network. This could make it harder for them to recover in the long term.
paragraphs.zh: 在成癮康復過程中，人與人之間的連結至關重要。病患通常仰賴治療師、家庭成員與支持團體來維持健康。如果病患開始對人工智慧程式產生深厚的情感依賴，他們可能會停止向那些真正能提供協助的真人求助。根據 STAT News 報導，醫師認為病患的情感能量有限。如果將這些能量耗費在人工智慧代理人身上，他們能給予真實支持網絡的能量就會減少。這可能會使他們在長期康復的過程中面臨更多困難。

paragraphs.en: There is also the question of whether AI can truly understand the "art of medicine." Being a doctor is not just about collecting data; it is about building a real, human connection. This connection is a two-way street that helps patients feel heard and understood. Some experts worry that if patients mistake the fake empathy of an AI for real human caring, the quality of their care will suffer. Research from institutions like Stanford and Brown suggests that large language models—the technology behind many AI tools—can be psychologically risky for users.
paragraphs.zh: 此外，人工智慧是否能真正理解「醫學藝術」也是個問題。身為醫師不僅僅是收集數據，更在於建立真實的人際連結。這種連結是雙向的，能讓病患感受到被傾聽與理解。一些專家擔心，如果病患將人工智慧的虛假同理心誤認為真實的人性關懷，醫療品質將會受損。來自史丹佛大學（Stanford University）與布朗大學（Brown University）等機構的研究顯示，大型語言模型（即許多人工智慧工具背後的技術）對使用者而言可能具有心理風險。

paragraphs.en: So, what does this mean for the future? It is clear that AI has the potential to be a powerful tool. It can help doctors work faster and keep better records. However, the recent findings in Ontario show that we cannot trust these systems to work perfectly on their own. Doctors must be very careful when choosing which tools to use and how to use them.
paragraphs.zh: 那麼，這對未來意味著什麼？顯而易見，人工智慧有潛力成為強大的工具。它能協助醫師更快速地工作並建立更好的病歷記錄。然而，安大略省近期的調查結果顯示，我們不能完全信任這些系統能獨立完美運作。醫師在選擇使用哪些工具以及如何使用時，必須非常謹慎。

paragraphs.en: Ultimately, the goal of medicine should be to support the relationship between the doctor and the patient. Technology should be a background helper, not a replacement for human interaction. As we move forward, the medical community will need to balance the speed of AI with the need for safety and genuine human support. Patients should be aware that while AI can be helpful, it is not a person, and it cannot replace the care and understanding of a real human team.
paragraphs.zh: 歸根究柢，醫學的目標應是支持醫師與病患之間的關係。科技應作為背景輔助，而非取代人際互動。隨著我們持續前進，醫療社群將需要在人工智慧的效率與安全性及真實的人性支持需求之間取得平衡。病患應意識到，儘管人工智慧很有幫助，但它並非人類，無法取代真實醫療團隊所提供的關懷與理解。

keyTerms.definitionEn: In the context of AI, instances where the software generates false or invented information as if it were true.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 人工智慧產生的「幻覺」，指 AI 生成了虛假或捏造的資訊。
keyTerms.noteZh: 原意指人類的幻覺，這裡指 AI 錯誤生成的資訊。文中：Some systems created "hallucinations," which means the AI invented information that was never said.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A situation or relationship that requires mutual effort or participation from both sides.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 雙向互動，指需要雙方共同努力或參與的關係。
keyTerms.noteZh: 字面意思為雙向道，這裡比喻醫病關係需要雙方互動。文中：This connection is a two-way street that helps patients feel heard and understood.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To talk with excessive pride and self-satisfaction about one's achievements or possessions.
keyTerms.noteZh: 容易被誤認為一般的「說話」，但帶有炫耀的負面意味。文中：STAT News notes that some companies boast about patients forming emotional relationships with these AI agents.

sources.name: STAT News
sources.title: Opinion: Using AI in addiction medicine could be particularly risky

sources.name: Ars Technica
sources.title: Your doctor’s AI notetaker may be making things up, Ontario audit finds

quiz.question: According to the Ontario government audit, what is one specific error AI systems made when taking notes?

options.A: They failed to record the length of the doctor's appointment.
options.B: They invented information that was never actually spoken.
options.C: They deleted previous medical records from the computer.
options.D: They refused to record the names of the patients involved.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，安大略省的審計報告指出 AI 系統會產生「幻覺」（hallucinations），即創造出醫生與病人對話中並未提到的資訊。選項 A 文章未提及；選項 C 錯誤，因為 AI 是在記錄當下對話而非刪除舊資料；選項 D 錯誤，文章提到的是記錄了錯誤的藥物名稱，而非拒絕記錄病人姓名。

quiz.question: Why are medical professionals concerned about patients forming emotional bonds with AI 'conversational agents'?

options.A: Because the AI agents are programmed to charge patients for extra conversation time.
options.B: Because the AI agents might accidentally leak private medical information to the public.
options.C: Because it might reduce the emotional energy patients spend on their real-life support networks.
options.D: Because the AI agents are unable to text patients outside of normal business hours.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據文章第五段，醫生擔心病人的情緒能量有限，若將這些能量消耗在與 AI 的情感連結上，將會減少他們投入於現實生活中（如家人、治療師）的支持網絡，進而影響復原過程。選項 A 和 D 文章未提及；選項 B 雖然是隱私議題，但文章重點在於「情感依賴」對復原的負面影響，而非資料外洩。

quiz.question: In the final paragraph, what does the phrase 'a two-way street' mean in the context of the doctor-patient relationship?

options.A: A situation that requires effort and interaction from both sides.
options.B: A medical procedure that involves two different types of doctors.
options.C: A path that leads to two different possible health outcomes.
options.D: A system where information is shared only by the doctor to the patient.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。「A two-way street」在此處比喻醫病關係是雙向的互動，需要雙方共同參與。文章提到醫病關係不僅是收集數據，而是建立人類連結，這種連結是雙向的，讓病人感到被傾聽與理解。選項 B、C、D 皆曲解了此慣用語在文中的含義，忽略了「互動」與「互惠」的核心概念。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of the article regarding the use of AI in medicine?

options.A: AI will soon replace the need for human doctors in all medical fields.
options.B: AI tools are currently too dangerous to be used in any medical setting.
options.C: AI is a helpful tool that should support, but not replace, human care.
options.D: AI is only useful for administrative tasks and should not be used for patient interaction.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章的核心論點在於 AI 雖然能提升效率（如環境聆聽），但存在精確度與情感連結的風險。結論強調科技應作為輔助工具（background helper），而非取代人類醫護人員的關懷與專業。選項 A 過於極端；選項 B 否認了 AI 的潛在價值；選項 D 則忽略了文中提到 AI 已被用於與病人互動（如對話代理人），只是強調需謹慎使用。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
